content.full_text:
Farn Porrrrcal Pnacrrcps CouurssroN
P.O. Box 807 .428 J Street . Sacramenro. CA 95g12-0g07
(916) 322-5660 . Fax (916) 322-0886
April24,2001
Robert Brennan, Councilmember
City of Arvin
Post Ofiice Box 463
Arvin, CA93203
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-01-090
Dear Mr. Brennan:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding your duties as an
Arvin City Council member under the conflict-of-interest proririonr 6f tt. political
Reform Act (the "Act").r Please note that nothing in this Ltter should be construed to
comment on any conduct or decisions that have already taken place.
QUESTTON
As a council member, may you participate in decisions of the Arvin City Council
that may materially affect your domestic partner, his business, or sources of iniome to his
business?
CONCLUSION
Based on the facts which you provided, you may participate in decisions that
materially affect your domestic partner, his business, and/or sources of income to his
business.
FACTS
You are a council member in the City of Arvin. You accepted your appointment
to the Arvin City Council in November 2000. On December 5, ZOOO,th.r. *u, an issue
on the council agenda regarding FEMA and reclassifying an existing flood zone. Since
your domestic partner, Randy Thompson, owns an insuranc. ug.rrliin the city of Arvin,
I Govemment Code sections 81000 - gl0l4. Commission regulations appear at Tifle 2, sections
I 8 I 09- I 8996, of the California Code of Regulations.


File No. A-01-090
Page No. 2
you abstained from voting because you felt there might be a conflict of interest. Your
domestic partner's agency has many clients in the City of Arvin who patronize his
business for flood insurance. You are not an employee of your domestic partner's
business nor are you a licensed insurance agent or broker.
Presently, there is another issue in regard to insurance before the Arvin City
Council. You are currently negotiating the renewal of contract for Police Chief Terry
Freeman, who is also a personal client of your domestic partner's insurance agency. The
Mayor of Arvin, Juan olivares, is pursuing you to abstain from partaking in the
negotiations of the police chief s contract bbcause of your prior abstention on the
insurance issue (FEMA). He feels that it is a conflict of interest because the police chief
is a client of your domestic partner's insurance agency. Since your domestic partner has
the only insurance agency in the City of Arvin, he carries policies for many citizens,
including city staff
ANALYSIS
Section 87100 of the Act prohibits a public official from making, parricipating in
making, or otherwise using his or her official position to influence a governmenial
decision in which the ofiicial has a financial interest. In brder to determine whether the
prohibition in section 87100 applies to a given decision, Regulation 18700 sets out the
following eight-step analysis.
1. Public Official
The conflict-of-interest provisions apply only to public ofiicials. (Section 87100).
As a member of the Arvin city council, you are a "public official,, subject to the
conflict-of-interest provisions of the Act. (Section g204g; Regulation iszorlu; ;
2. Conduct Covered
The conflict-of-interest provisions cover specific conduct: making, participating
in making, or attempting to use one's official position to influence a govlrnmentil
decision. (Section 87100). Discussing and voting on a personnel contract is considered
laking, or participating in making, a governmental decision, and is therefore regulated
by the Act. (Regulation t87}2.t(a)(:) )
3. Identifying Relevant Economic Interests
The Act's conflict-oflinierest provisions apply only to conflicts of interest arising
from economic interests. The "economic interests'; from which conflicts of interest may
arise are defined in Section 87103 and Regulations 18703-18703.5. There are six kinds
of such economic interests. Your questions suggest the possible application of four of
them.
l. A public official has an economic interest in a business entity in which he or she has a
direct or indirectinvestment of $2,000 or more. (Section azio:(a); Regulation
18703.1(a) ) An indirect investment means, among other thingr, uny irivestment


File No. A-01-090
Page No. 3
owned by the oflicial's immediate family. (Section 87103.) Section 82029 defines
"immediate family" as "spouse and dependent children." Since Mr. Thompson is
neither your spouse nor dependent child, this section is not implicated.
2. A public official has an economic interest in any source of income, including
promised income, which aggregates to $500 or more within l2 months prior to the
decision. (Section 87103(c); Regulation 13703.3.) Section 82030 defines "income"
to include "any community property interest in the income of a spouse." For
purposes of the Political Reform Act, you do not have a "community property''
interest in income to Mr. Thompson. (DeRosa Advice Letter No. I-98-284, copy
enclosed.) Further, you have not disclosed any payment from Mr. Thompson, such as
a loan to you, which results in income to you.
3. A public official has an economic interest in any source of gifts to him or her if the
gifts aggregate to $320 or more within 12 months prior to the decision. (Section
87103(e);Regulation 18703.4.) Howeveq gifts in a bona fide dating relationship or
between spouses are disregarded. (Section 82028(a)(3); Albuquerque Advice Letter,
No. A-00-120.)
4. A public ofiicial has an economic interest in his or h'er personal finances (expenses,
income, assets, or liabilities), as well as those of his or her immediate family.
(Section 87103; Regulation 18703.5.) Mr. Thompson is not a part of your
"immediate family" as defined in the Act. Thus, financial effects on Mr. Thompson
caused by your governmental decisions will not result in a conflict of interest for you
Absent an "economic interest" as defined in the Act, you will not have a conflict
of interest under the Act. Please note that our advice issued in response to your request
is limited to obligations arising under the Act. You may wish to contact the Attorney
General's Office with respect to other provisions of law zuch as Government Code
Section 1090.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660
Sincerely
Luisa Menchaca
Counsel
John W. Wallace
Senior Counsel, Legal Division
Enclosures
